methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra, having raised $11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including Series A-stage companies such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to the next stage, implies a valuation of $29M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). The peer-median approach reflects valuations at Series A, one stage ahead of Karman's current position.

The triangulated analysis produces a valuation range of $24M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M across the two methods. The wide range reflects fundamental uncertainty regarding whether Karman's risk and opportunity profile aligns more closely with early-stage capital multiples or with the valuations commanded by its more mature peer set. Key sensitivities include the company's progress toward Series A milestones and comparative assessment of its technology position relative to named peers.
